Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. 15 Years of Forking 58 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. New Apple Silicon M4 and M5 HiDPI Limitation on 4K External Displays 43 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 209 upvotes, 40 comments
  4. Show HN: QuickBEAM – run JavaScript as supervised Erlang/OTP processes 85 upvotes, 13 comments
  5. Kyushu Railway Company Train Varieties 64 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. More on Version Control 61 upvotes, 11 comments
  7. I'll buy your electronics to feed our robot 61 upvotes, 12 comments
  8. About the Atmosphere 30 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 119 upvotes, 30 comments
  10. There is No Spoon. A software engineers primer for demystified ML 65 upvotes, 15 comments
  11. Interview: Nobonoko, Master of the Minimal Sequencer 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. The rise and fall of IBM's 4 Pi aerospace computers: an illustrated history 78 upvotes, 24 comments
  13. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 412 upvotes, 173 comments
  14. The road signs that teach travellers about France 60 upvotes, 18 comments
  15. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics 519 upvotes, 235 comments
  16. Show HN: Crazierl – An Erlang Operating System 46 upvotes, 14 comments
  17. The Cognitive Dark Forest 288 upvotes, 129 comments
  18. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 70 upvotes, 25 comments
  19. Neovim 0.12.0 303 upvotes, 145 comments
  20. Ohm's Peg-to-WASM Compiler 42 upvotes, 15 comments
  21. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 635 upvotes, 370 comments
  22. Claude Code runs Git reset –hard origin/main against project repo every 10 mins 191 upvotes, 121 comments
  23. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 295 upvotes, 212 comments
  24. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 360 upvotes, 274 comments
  25. Creating West Coast Buddhism (2024) 54 upvotes, 37 comments
  26. C++26 is done ISO C++ standards meeting, Trip Report 171 upvotes, 143 comments
  27. Coding Agents Could Make Free Software Matter Again 117 upvotes, 114 comments
  28. Quantum Control Plane 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. The Sudden Fall of OpenAI's Most Hyped Product Since ChatGPT 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. DoesItAgeVerify: The age verification status of Open Source Operating Systems 19 upvotes, 13 comments

Last generated 11:00am, 30 March 2026. Source here.
